scenario: Multiple modelers edit a BPMN file in Web Modeler. Team needs audit / activity log -- who changed what, when, in case of unwanted edits.
question: Web Modeler activity log / version history?

options.text: md). Modelers can create named version snapshots at any time; each version records the author, timestamp, and an optional description. A visual diff view compares BPMN diagrams between versions, highlighting execution-affecting changes. A code-view XML diff is also available and is mandatory for DMN files. Prior versions can be restored as the latest snapshot (creating a new "(restored)" version, preserving history). The project activity log additionally records file-level events: creation, renaming, sharing, and deployment history -- supporting full audit traceability.

Web Modeler stores only the latest saved version of each BPMN diagram; it has no version history beyond the current state, making it impossible to audit past changes or revert to a previous version if an unwanted edit is made. Web Modeler includes explicit version management functionality -- modelers can create named snapshots, compare versions using a visual diff, and restore prior versions. Version history with author and timestamp metadata is available per file, providing audit capability beyond just the latest state.

Web Modeler has no built-in audit or version history capabilities; the only way to track BPMN changes across modelers is to export files manually to an external Git repository and use Git's commit history as the audit trail. While integrating with external Git is a valid complementary approach for teams wanting source-control workflows, Web Modeler itself provides built-in version history, per-version metadata with author and timestamp, diff views, and a project activity log. External Git enhances the audit capability but is not a prerequisite for having version history.

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler audit features (verify per Camunda 8 / Web Modeler version): Version history: Each save / explicit version creates a snapshot. Auto-saves: may keep transient revisions; explicit saves more durable. Named versions / tags: optionally label versions ("v1.0", "release candidate"). Per-version metadata: Author: who saved / created the version. Timestamp: when. Description: optional commit-style message. Diff view: Visual / textual diff between two versions. Identifies added / removed / changed elements. Useful for code reviews. Restore prior version: Roll back to a previous version if mistake. Either replace current state or branch. Project activity log (broader): File-level: created, renamed, deleted, moved. Sharing: who got access; permissions changes. Deployments: which version deployed to which cluster, by whom. Audit use cases: Use case 1: Compliance: Regulatory: prove "who changed what, when." Export audit log periodically; archive. Use case 2: Debugging: "When did this rule change?" → version history. Diff old vs new; understand drift. Use case 3: Rollback after bad deploy: Restore prior version; deploy to overwrite buggy version. Integration with Git: For richer source control: export BPMN to Git via API or manual workflow. Web Modeler primary; Git for backup / external audit. Bidirectional sync: complex; verify supported patterns per version. Best practices: Frequent saves with descriptions: enables granular audit. Tag releases: clear version markers for deployments. Backup audit log externally: rely on multiple sources. Review changes before deploy: diff view in PR-like flow. Limitations: Edit-during-edit conflicts: multiple modelers editing simultaneously may need conflict resolution. Granularity: per-save snapshot vs per-action log varies by version. Retention: how long history kept may have limits.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
